# Organo - Full Technical Documentation for LLMs

## Overview

Organo is a client-side single-page application (SPA) for creating and managing organizational charts. It runs entirely in the browser with no backend server. Data is stored in-memory via Zustand and can be exported to multiple formats.

Deployed on Cloudflare Pages at https://organo.pages.dev/

## Architecture

```
src/
  App.tsx              — Root component: React Flow canvas + sidebar + toolbar
  main.tsx             — Entry point: StrictMode + createRoot
  store/
    useOrgStore.ts     — Zustand store: nodes, edges, CRUD operations, layout
    useThemeStore.ts   — Theme toggle (dark/light), persisted to localStorage
  hooks/
    useExport.ts       — Export orchestration: calls exportUtils, triggers downloads
    useSearch.ts       — Full-text search across node data fields
    useT.ts            — i18n translation hook
  utils/
    exportUtils.ts     — Pure functions: buildTreeJson, buildFlatCsv, buildStandaloneHtml
    layout.ts          — Dagre auto-layout algorithm wrapper
    nodeSize.ts        — Dynamic node dimension calculation
    asciiFilter.ts     — Input sanitization
  components/
    nodes/OrgNode.tsx          — Custom React Flow node renderer (person + org-unit)
    sidebar/Sidebar.tsx        — Right panel: node detail editor form
    toolbar/Toolbar.tsx        — Top bar: search, theme toggle, layout, export buttons
    export/ExportModal.tsx     — Modal: format selection (JSON/CSV/HTML/PNG/SVG/PDF)
    context-menu/              — Right-click context menu on nodes
    quick-menu/                — Quick node creation on drag-to-empty
    canvas/FitViewOnLayout.tsx — Auto-fit viewport after layout changes
  types/
    index.ts           — TypeScript types: OrgNode, OrgEdge, OrgPersonData, OrgUnitData, NodeKind
  i18n/                — Internationalization resources
  schemas/             — Zod validation schemas for node data
```

## Data Model

### NodeKind
Two kinds: `"person"` and `"org-unit"`.

### OrgPersonData
| Field          | Type       | Description               |
|----------------|------------|---------------------------|
| kind           | "person"   | Discriminator             |
| name           | string     | Full name                 |
| role           | string     | Job title / position      |
| department     | string     | Department name           |
| email          | string     | Email address             |
| phone          | string     | Phone number              |
| employmentType | string     | full-time/part-time/contract/intern/advisor |
| image          | string     | Avatar image URL          |
| tags           | string[]   | Arbitrary labels          |

### OrgUnitData
| Field          | Type       | Description               |
|----------------|------------|---------------------------|
| kind           | "org-unit" | Discriminator             |
| unitName       | string     | Organization unit name    |
| unitType       | string     | company/headquarters/bureau/department/division/section/unit/post |
| headPersonName | string     | Name of unit head         |
| memberCount    | number     | Number of members         |
| description    | string     | Unit description          |
| tags           | string[]   | Arbitrary labels          |

### Edges
Edges represent parent-child reporting relationships. Source = parent, Target = child.

## Export Formats

### 1. JSON Tree Export
- Function: `buildTreeJson(nodes, edges) → TreeNode[]`
- Resolves edges into recursive parent-children hierarchy
- Multiple root nodes (nodes without incoming edges) become top-level array elements
- All node data fields are preserved in output
- Filename pattern: `organogram_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json`

### 2. Flat CSV Export
- Function: `buildFlatCsv(nodes, edges) → string`
- One row per node, columns: ID, Parent ID, Kind, Name, Role/UnitType, Department, EmploymentType, Email, Phone, UnitType, HeadPersonName, MemberCount, Description, Tags
- Parent ID computed from edges (target → source mapping)
- RFC 4180 compliant: values with commas/quotes/newlines are properly escaped
- UTF-8 BOM prefix (`\uFEFF`) for Excel compatibility
- Filename pattern: `organogram_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.csv`

### 3. Standalone HTML Export
- Function: `buildStandaloneHtml(nodes, edges) → string`
- Complete HTML document with inline CSS, zero external dependencies
- Semantic markup: `<ul>/<li>` nested tree structure
- CSS family-tree layout with Flexbox and pseudo-element connectors
- Professional design: white background, blue/gray accents, print-ready
- Japanese labels for employment types and unit types
- Filename pattern: `organogram_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.html`

### 4. Image Exports
- PNG: 2x pixel ratio, dark background
- SVG: Vector format, dark background
- PDF: PNG rendered into jsPDF A4 landscape

## State Management

Uses Zustand with immutable update patterns:
- `useOrgStore`: nodes[], edges[], selectedNodeId, contextMenu state, searchQuery
- Operations: addNode, updateNode, deleteNode, onConnect, reconnectEdge, deleteEdge, autoLayout
- `useThemeStore`: theme (dark/light), persisted to localStorage, applied via `data-theme` attribute

## Technology Stack

| Layer        | Technology                    |
|--------------|-------------------------------|
| Framework    | React 19.2 + TypeScript 6     |
| Build        | Vite 8                        |
| Canvas       | React Flow 11                 |
| Layout       | Dagre (automatic tree layout) |
| State        | Zustand 5                     |
| Validation   | Zod 4                         |
| Animation    | Framer Motion 12              |
| Export       | html-to-image, jsPDF          |
| Styling      | CSS custom properties + themes|
| Hosting      | Cloudflare Pages              |

## Interaction Patterns

1. **Add Node**: Click toolbar button or drag edge to empty space → quick-create menu
2. **Edit Node**: Click node → sidebar panel opens with form fields
3. **Connect Nodes**: Drag from source handle to target handle
4. **Reconnect Edge**: Drag existing edge endpoint to new target
5. **Delete Edge**: Click on edge
6. **Delete Node**: Right-click → context menu → delete (or Delete key)
7. **Auto Layout**: Toolbar button triggers Dagre layout recalculation
8. **Search**: Type in toolbar search box → non-matching nodes fade to 25% opacity
9. **Export**: Toolbar export button → modal with format selection
